data.title: CrossFit MINS Workout
data.text: Workout of the Day 26.10.2025 WODDAILYSWEAT For Quality 6 Rounds 3:00 Row/Ski Erg 20 Box Stepups 15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16) 10 Ring Rows Sweat: 16/8 Train: 20/12 Compete: 24/16"
data.full_text:
Workout of the Day

26.10.2025

WOD
DAILYSWEAT


For Quality
6 Rounds
3:00 Row/Ski Erg
20 Box Stepups
15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16)
10 Ring Rows


Sweat: 16/8
Train: 20/12
Compete: 24/16"

ai_explanation.explanation:
Workout Overview


Hey everyone! Today’s WOD is all about quality movement and building endurance. We’ll be doing 6 rounds of a mix of cardio, strength, and bodyweight exercises. The goal here is to maintain good form throughout each movement while keeping a steady pace. This workout is designed to challenge your cardiovascular system and muscular endurance, so let’s break it down step by step!


Workout Components


1. Row/Ski Erg (3:00)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • For the rower, sit with your feet strapped in, grip the handle, and start with your knees bent and back straight.
  • Push through your legs, lean back slightly, and pull the handle towards your chest, keeping your elbows close.
  • Reverse the motion: extend your arms, lean forward, and bend your knees to return to the starting position.
  • For the Ski Erg, stand tall, grip the handles, and pull down with your arms while driving your knees up slightly, mimicking a skiing motion.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Keep your core engaged to protect your back.
  • Use your legs to drive the movement, not just your arms.
  • Maintain a smooth, controlled rhythm.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • If you’re new to rowing, focus on shorter intervals (1:00) or use a lower resistance setting.
  • For the Ski Erg, adjust the height of the handles if needed for comfort.

    • 2. Box Step-Ups (20 reps)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • Stand facing the box with your feet hip-width apart.
  • Step up with one foot, pressing through your heel to lift your body onto the box.
  • Bring your other foot up to meet the first, then step back down one foot at a time.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Keep your chest up and engage your core.
  • Drive through your heel to activate your glutes.
  • Alternate legs for each rep to ensure balance.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • Use a lower box if you’re not comfortable with the height.
  • Perform step-ups without weights or use a plate for added challenge.

    • 3. Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (15 reps)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width, toes slightly pointed out, and the kettlebell on the ground between your feet.
  • Hinge at the hips to grab the kettlebell with both hands.
  • Stand up, driving through your heels, and as you reach standing, pull the kettlebell up towards your chin, keeping your elbows high.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Keep your back straight and chest up during the lift.
  • Use your legs to initiate the movement, not just your arms.
  • Ensure the kettlebell stays close to your body throughout the pull.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • Use a lighter kettlebell or perform the movement without weight to focus on form.
  • If you have shoulder issues, consider a kettlebell deadlift instead.

    • 4. Ring Rows (10 reps)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • Set the rings at a height that allows you to lean back comfortably while holding onto the rings.
  • Start in a lean-back position, arms extended, and pull your chest towards the rings by bending your elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
  • Lower yourself back to the starting position with control.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Keep your body straight from head to heels; avoid sagging your hips.
  • Engage your core throughout the movement.
  • Focus on pulling with your back, not just your arms.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • Adjust the height of the rings to make the movement easier or harder.
  • If rings are not available, substitute with inverted rows on a barbell or TRX.

    • Loading and Rep-Modification Options


  • **Sweat:**
  • Row/Ski Erg: 2:00, Box Step-Ups: 16/8, Kettlebell: 12/8, Ring Rows: 8 reps.

  • **Train:**
  • Row/Ski Erg: 3:00, Box Step-Ups: 20/12, Kettlebell: 16/12, Ring Rows: 10 reps.

  • **Compete:**
  • Row/Ski Erg: 3:00, Box Step-Ups: 24/16, Kettlebell: 24/16, Ring Rows: 10 reps.

    • Strategy Advice

  • Focus on maintaining a steady pace throughout the workout. It’s better to keep moving at a manageable speed than to go all out and burn out quickly.
  • Break up the reps into smaller sets if you start to feel fatigued, especially on the kettlebell pulls and ring rows.
  • Remember to breathe and stay relaxed; quality over quantity is key today!

    • Alright, team! Let’s get ready to crush this WOD together. Keep your form tight, listen to your body, and most importantly, have fun!

combined_content.title: CrossFit MINS Workout
combined_content.workout_text:
WOD:


For Quality

6 Rounds:

  • 3:00 Row/Ski Erg
  • 20 Box Step-ups
  • 15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16)
  • 10 Ring Rows

    • Sweat: 16/8

    Train: 20/12

    Compete: 24/16

--- AI EXPLANATION --- ## Workout Overview Hey everyone! Today’s WOD is all about quality movement and building endurance. We’ll be doing 6 rounds of a mix of cardio, strength, and bodyweight exercises. The goal here is to maintain good form throughout each movement while keeping a steady pace. This workout is designed to challenge your cardiovascular system and muscular endurance, so let’s break it down step by step! ## Workout Components ### 1. Row/Ski Erg (3:00) - **Technique Steps:** - For the rower, sit with your feet strapped in, grip the handle, and start with your knees bent and back straight. - Push through your legs, lean back slightly, and pull the handle towards your chest, keeping your elbows close. - Reverse the motion: extend your arms, lean forward, and bend your knees to return to the starting position. - For the Ski Erg, stand tall, grip the handles, and pull down with your arms while driving your knees up slightly, mimicking a skiing motion. - **Form Tips:** - Keep your core engaged to protect your back. - Use your legs to drive the movement, not just your arms. - Maintain a smooth, controlled rhythm. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - If you’re new to rowing, focus on shorter intervals (1:00) or use a lower resistance setting. - For the Ski Erg, adjust the height of the handles if needed for comfort. ### 2. Box Step-Ups (20 reps) - **Technique Steps:** - Stand facing the box with your feet hip-width apart. - Step up with one foot, pressing through your heel to lift your body onto the box. - Bring your other foot up to meet the first, then step back down one foot at a time. - **Form Tips:** - Keep your chest up and engage your core. - Drive through your heel to activate your glutes. - Alternate legs for each rep to ensure balance. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - Use a lower box if you’re not comfortable with the height. - Perform step-ups without weights or use a plate for added challenge. ### 3. Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (15 reps) - **Technique Steps:** - Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width, toes slightly pointed out, and the kettlebell on the ground between your feet. - Hinge at the hips to grab the kettlebell with both hands. - Stand up, driving through your heels, and as you reach standing, pull the kettlebell up towards your chin, keeping your elbows high. - **Form Tips:** - Keep your back straight and chest up during the lift. - Use your legs to initiate the movement, not just your arms. - Ensure the kettlebell stays close to your body throughout the pull. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - Use a lighter kettlebell or perform the movement without weight to focus on form. - If you have shoulder issues, consider a kettlebell deadlift instead. ### 4. Ring Rows (10 reps) - **Technique Steps:** - Set the rings at a height that allows you to lean back comfortably while holding onto the rings. - Start in a lean-back position, arms extended, and pull your chest towards the rings by bending your elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together. - Lower yourself back to the starting position with control. - **Form Tips:** - Keep your body straight from head to heels; avoid sagging your hips. - Engage your core throughout the movement. - Focus on pulling with your back, not just your arms. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - Adjust the height of the rings to make the movement easier or harder. - If rings are not available, substitute with inverted rows on a barbell or TRX. ## Loading and Rep-Modification Options - **Sweat:** - Row/Ski Erg: 2:00, Box Step-Ups: 16/8, Kettlebell: 12/8, Ring Rows: 8 reps. - **Train:** - Row/Ski Erg: 3:00, Box Step-Ups: 20/12, Kettlebell: 16/12, Ring Rows: 10 reps. - **Compete:** - Row/Ski Erg: 3:00, Box Step-Ups: 24/16, Kettlebell: 24/16, Ring Rows: 10 reps. ### Strategy Advice - Focus on maintaining a steady pace throughout the workout. It’s better to keep moving at a manageable speed than to go all out and burn out quickly. - Break up the reps into smaller sets if you start to feel fatigued, especially on the kettlebell pulls and ring rows. - Remember to breathe and stay relaxed; quality over quantity is key today! Alright, team! Let’s get ready to crush this WOD together. Keep your form tight, listen to your body, and most importantly, have fun!
